issues.detail: REQ-014 says "completion-credit-only public reputation" and specifically forbids a "public profile page or badge display" and public star/numerical ratings. AT-014.03 invents the broader rule that completion credit "has no public display in v1." The requirement makes the credit-earned confirmation private, not necessarily every representation of completion credit.
issues.fix: Remove the blanket no-public-display assertion. Test only the stated prohibitions: no public volunteer-profile page, badge display, star rating, or numerical volunteer rating.

issues.detail: The qualifiers in "current projects ... in-progress tasks, unresolved blockers/clarifications" lack negative coverage. AT-014.01 proves required fixtures appear but would still pass if completed/cancelled projects, queued/done tasks, or resolved blockers and clarifications also appeared.
issues.fix: Use mixed fixtures and assert that only current projects, in-progress tasks, and unresolved blockers/clarifications appear in those dashboard sections.

issues.detail: The mapped clause includes "durable, append-only ... tamper-evident," but AT-014.04 only reads one event and rejects ordinary overwrite/update/delete operations. That does not prove persistence across a storage boundary, successful appending of later per-project events, or detection of tampering that bypasses the normal mutation API.
issues.fix: Add observable probes that the event survives a service/storage reload, a second project completion appends a new event without changing the first, and controlled out-of-band corruption is detected rather than silently accepted.

issues.detail: The coverage map claims the "first-tool eligibility" field is covered, but AT-014.04 merely requires the field to be carried and AT-014.05 never asserts its value. An implementation setting it always true, always false, or arbitrarily could pass.
issues.fix: Complete two projects for the same volunteer and assert the first event has the expected first-tool eligibility while the later event does not.

issues.detail: The row "'Portfolio already exists on GitHub' — framing ... no test needed [cross: AT-008.03]" delegates too much. AT-008.03 verifies that a validated, non-hidden repository is public and MIT-licensed, but it does not verify REQ-014's stated "credit recorded from day one" framing.
issues.fix: Keep the public-MIT fact delegated to AT-008.03, but add a REQ-014 assertion that the volunteer-facing credit copy uses the required recorded-from-day-one framing.
